description: Refactor AsyncProduceStrategy in xtuner/v1/rl/agent_loop/producer.py so non-colocated rollout can keep pending work across calls, stop on update/expiry signals, and only produce into the replay buffer.
steps: Step 1: change AsyncProduceStrategy.produce_batch() to return a ProduceBatchStatus and only schedule/recycle rollout groups into the replay buffer instead of returning training batches directly
Step 2: move pending rollout tasks from a per-call local variable to self._pending_tasks so unfinished work survives across multiple produce_batch() calls
Step 3: expose cleanup_pending_tasks() as a public strategy API and remove the current automatic cleanup from the end of produce_batch()
Step 4: preserve oversample, tail-batch, partial-rollout, and stale-sample behavior under the new status-based flow, including immediate EXPIRED_BATCH stop semantics
Step 5: write per-group generation timing onto RolloutState.extra_fields and return pause_time_s separately so timing can be reconstructed when trainer batches are consumed
Step 6: add or update producer tests that cover NORMAL, UPDATE_ABORT, EXPIRED_BATCH, cross-call pending-task retention, and explicit cleanup behavior

description: Align ProduceStrategy base class plus SyncProduceStrategy and other strategy subclasses with the new producer/consumer interface split so colocated and disaggregated paths share compatible strategy contracts.
steps: Step 1: update the ProduceStrategy base class interface so strategy implementations can participate in the split produce-to-buffer, explicit-cleanup, and status-driven manager flow
Step 2: adjust SyncProduceStrategy to match the new strategy contract while preserving the current colocated semantics and batch production behavior
Step 3: update any remaining ProduceStrategy subclasses or helper call sites to use the same method signatures, return conventions, and cleanup expectations
Step 4: add or update focused tests that confirm the shared strategy interface still works for both colocated and disaggregated manager paths

description: Refactor AgentLoopManager in xtuner/v1/rl/agent_loop/agent_loop_manager.py into a split producer/consumer state machine that supports both colocated produce_batch() and background disaggregated production.
steps: Step 1: add ProduceBatchStatus support to ProduceBatchResult and add manager runtime state for _status, _update_event, _finish_event, _model_rollout_step, and _pause_time_s
Step 2: implement _produce_batch_to_buffer() for single-task and multi-task cases, including task batch allocation and status aggregation priority UPDATE_ABORT > EXPIRED_BATCH > NORMAL
Step 3: implement _get_batch_from_buffer() so trainer consumption reconstructs leftover counts and generation timing stats from replay-buffer contents and cached pause_time_s

Step 4: add cleanup_pending_tasks(for_weight_update=False), reset(model_rollout_step), produce_loop(batch_size, start_rollout_step), and get_batch(batch_size, rollout_step) with the state transitions defined in design/disagg_design.md

Step 5: adapt the existing colocated produce_batch() path to reuse continue_generation -> _produce_batch_to_buffer() -> cleanup_pending_tasks(for_weight_update=False) -> _get_batch_from_buffer() -> pause_generation
Step 6: add or update manager tests for single-task and multi-task behavior, EXPIRED_BATCH empty-batch returns, cleanup event ordering, and producer-loop stop/reset semantics

description: Update rollout-step and staleness plumbing in xtuner/v1/rl/agent_loop/single_turn_agent_loop.py and xtuner/v1/rl/agent_loop/utils.py so samples record rollout model version, not producer loop iteration.
steps: Step 1: thread model_rollout_step through the producer and manager call chain into SingleTurnAgentLoop.generate_sample() and PartialRolloutHandler.postprocess()
Step 2: change partial-rollout postprocessing so response_rollout_steps and seq_staleness are derived from model_rollout_step semantics instead of per-call rollout_step semantics
Step 3: add a lightweight refresh_seq_staleness(group, current_rollout_step) helper for re-checking buffered samples at consume time
Step 4: keep partial-rollout continuation and EXPIRED reset behavior correct for COMPLETED, ABORTED, and EXPIRED groups under the new step semantics
Step 5: add or update focused tests for model_rollout_step recording, staleness refresh, and partial-rollout postprocess behavior

description: Update RLColocateTrainer in xtuner/v1/train/rl_colocate_trainer.py so the existing colocated RL training path stays compatible with the latest producer, manager, rollout-step changes, while still supporting AsyncProduceStrategy through coordinated trainer and strategy adaptation.
steps: Step 1: adapt RLColocateTrainer to the latest AgentLoopManager and ProduceStrategy contracts without changing the current colocated trainer entry semantics
Step 2: make sure RLColocateTrainer can still run with AsyncProduceStrategy on the colocated path, and update the trainer-side flow as needed to match the refactored producer and manager behavior
Step 3: add the corresponding strategy-side compatibility work needed so AsyncProduceStrategy remains usable by the colocated trainer instead of becoming disaggregated-only
Step 4: make sure the colocated training flow still handles batch production, training, evaluation, and weight-sync sequencing correctly after the shared producer and manager refactor
Step 5: keep RLColocateTrainer compatible with the latest rollout-step and staleness semantics introduced for shared rollout-state handling
Step 6: add or update focused tests that confirm RLColocateTrainer still runs correctly against the refactored shared interfaces, including AsyncProduceStrategy coverage on the colocated path

description: Add xtuner/v1/train/rl_disaggregated_trainer.py with RLDisaggregatedTrainerConfig and RLDisaggregatedTrainer that run a background producer loop and a foreground trainer loop without changing the current CLI contract.
steps: Step 1: add RLDisaggregatedTrainerConfig and RLDisaggregatedTrainer with a synchronous fit() wrapper over an async _fit() implementation that matches the existing example config shape
Step 2: start a background producer_task with agent_loop_manager.produce_loop() and make the foreground loop consume batches only through agent_loop_manager.get_batch()
Step 3: skip _prepare_train_data() and train_controller.fit() whenever get_batch() returns EXPIRED_BATCH, and go straight into the weight-sync path
Step 4: implement _sync_weights_and_save() so it calls cleanup_pending_tasks(for_weight_update=True), then checkpoint/save logic, then bind_train_rollout plus a fake_update_weights() placeholder
Step 5: keep evaluation higher priority than background rollout by running eval before agent_loop_manager.reset(model_rollout_step=rollout_step)
Step 6: add minimal trainer tests for EXPIRED_BATCH skip-train behavior, FINISH shutdown of the producer task, and eval-before-reset ordering

description: Extend checkpoint and resume behavior for the disaggregated path so saves only happen from a paused static point and resume rebuilds manager state safely before producer restart.
steps: Step 1: save checkpoints only after cleanup_pending_tasks(for_weight_update=True) has finished and before reset(model_rollout_step=rollout_step) runs
Step 2: extend AgentLoopManager.save() to persist agent_loop_manager_state.json with status and a model_rollout_step_override value, in addition to sampler state and replay buffer contents
Step 3: reject save or force cleanup if any AsyncProduceStrategy still has non-empty self._pending_tasks at checkpoint time
Step 4: extend AgentLoopManager.resume() to restore sampler state, replay buffer, and manager state into UPDATE_ABORT with reconstructed runtime events and cleared pause_time_s

Step 5: implement trainer-side resume flow so train state is restored first, rollout weights are re-synced from train, reset(model_rollout_step=saved_model_rollout_step) is called, and a fresh producer_task is created instead of restoring old tasks
Step 6: add checkpoint tests for save-before-reset semantics, paused resume state, pending-task safety checks, and one-time post-resume weight sync before producer restart

description: Add colocate trainer integration regression coverage for the shared producer/manager refactor so the existing colocated RL path stays correct while disaggregated support lands, with primary coverage focused on examples/v1/config/rl_grpo_gsm8k_judge.py and examples/v1/config/rl_multi_task_gsm8k_dapo_math.py.
steps: Step 1: extend the colocate trainer integration coverage to exercise the refactored AgentLoopManager and ProduceStrategy shared path under the existing rl_colocate_trainer flow
Step 2: focus the colocate integration coverage on examples/v1/config/rl_grpo_gsm8k_judge.py and examples/v1/config/rl_multi_task_gsm8k_dapo_math.py as the primary regression configs
Step 3: use zdev/rl_design_colocate.sh as the reference entry flow when deciding how the colocate integration test or smoke path should launch and wire environment variables

Step 4: verify the colocated training loop still produces batches, trains, syncs weights, and completes without regressions after the producer/manager interface split while keeping the test minimal but stable enough to catch regressions introduced by the new shared strategy and manager behavior

description: Wire the new disaggregated path through the existing draft configs and regression coverage so both single-task and multi-task examples build and run under the new trainer contract.
steps: Step 1: keep examples/v1/config/rl_disagg_single.py and examples/v1/config/rl_disagg_multi.py working with the new trainer/config implementation without changing their public config shape
Step 2: add the smallest useful automated coverage for the disaggregated single-task and multi-task paths, reusing existing RL test patterns and extending run_test.sh if needed
Step 3: verify multi-task task_batch_sizes and replay-buffer consumption remain correct under the disaggregated manager flow
Step 4: pass minimal end-to-end no-crash coverage for the two draft disaggregated example configs
